facts.text: Cannabis contains 483 known compounds, including at least 65 cannabinoids beyond THC.
Tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) is the main psychoactive component of cannabis.
When cannabis is eaten rather than smoked, effects can take up to 90 minutes to onset because the drug must be digested and absorbed.
The effects of cannabis last for two to six hours depending on the amount used.
At high doses, cannabis can cause hallucinations and delusions, including ideas of reference.
Cannabis is native to Central or South Asia.
The ancient Greeks called cannabis 'κάνναβις' and may have learned of its use by observing Scythian funerals.
In Akkadian, cannabis was known as 'qunubu'.
The Hebrew word for cannabis, 'qaneh bosem', was adopted from the Akkadian.
The Latin word 'cannabis' evolved through Proto-Germanic into the modern English word 'hemp'.
CBD is a 5-HT1A receptor agonist, placing it in the same receptor class as certain anti-anxiety compounds.
The commonly held belief that sativa strains are stimulating and indica strains are sedating is disputed by researchers.
A 2015 review found high CBD-to-THC cannabis strains were associated with lower risk of developing psychosis and better cognitive function.
High CBD-to-THC strains were also associated with a later age of onset of psychosis compared to low CBD-to-THC strains.
The origins of the word 'cannabis' are linked to a Scythian word.
Almost two-thirds of poly-drug users in the 2009–2010 Scottish Crime and Justice Survey used cannabis.
Cannabis research is complicated by the fact that many cannabis smokers also smoke tobacco, making it difficult to isolate the health effects of each.
The gateway hypothesis proposes that cannabis use increases the probability of trying harder drugs.
A Pew Research Center poll found political opposition to marijuana was significantly linked to concerns about health effects and children's access.
Cannabis produces an increase in appetite as one of its known physical effects.
Cannabis can impair short-term memory, balance, and fine psychomotor control.
When smoked, the onset of cannabis effects is felt within minutes.
Cannabis has been used as an entheogenic drug in various traditional medicines for centuries.
